services.title: Services
services.hint: 3D printing, CNC, laser, materials, and files

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.description: How to register, send us your work, submit manufacturing or design requests, and buy from the shop.

services.title: Services help
services.description: What to expect from 3D printing, CNC, and laser workflows, how materials are chosen, and which files to upload.

ordersAccount.title: Orders & account
ordersAccount.description: Order progress, payments, signing in, cancellations and refunds, and how your 3D designs are kept private.

createAccount.title: How do I create an account?
createAccount.body: Use Sign up / Create account from the header. Enter a valid email and password, complete any verification step if prompted, and sign in with the same credentials for the shop, service requests, and your customer portal. Keep your password secure; use password reset on the sign-in page if you forget it.

uploadDesign.title: How do I upload a design?
uploadDesign.body: For 3D-related flows, open the relevant service (for example 3D printing or your account’s My Designs area when available). Upload the file types requested on the form (commonly STL, STEP/STP, or OBJ for meshes—see Supported file types). Files are tied to your signed-in account so only you and our team can access them for fulfillment. If the uploader rejects a file, check format, size limits, and that the model is manifold or valid for the workflow.

submitRequest.title: How do I submit a service request?
submitRequest.body: Choose the service you need—3D printing, Design & CAD, laser, CNC, or a broader project—from the site navigation. Complete each step: specifications, quantities, material or process choices, and attachments. Review the summary, then submit. You will receive confirmation by email where configured, and you can track progress from your account requests or order area depending on the product type.

placeOrder.title: How do I place a shop order?
placeOrder.body: Browse the catalog, open a product, choose options and quantity, and add to cart. Open the cart, apply a coupon if you have one, then proceed to checkout. Enter shipping and billing details, choose payment method, and confirm. After payment succeeds, the order appears under My orders with status updates as we process and ship it.

trackOrder.title: How do I track my order?
trackOrder.body: Sign in and go to My orders (or Account → Orders). Open the order to see its current status, totals, and addresses. When the order ships, tracking information may appear there and in shipment emails. If something looks stuck, contact support with your order number.

print3dGuide.title: How does 3D printing work on MABA?
print3dGuide.body: You describe the part, choose material and options where offered, and upload your model. We review manufacturability, may suggest changes, then schedule production. Lead time depends on material, queue, and complexity. You will see status updates from receipt through production and shipping. For questions about orientation, supports, or tolerances, note them in your request or contact us.

cncGuide.title: What should I know for CNC requests?
cncGuide.body: Provide accurate CAD or drawings (often STEP, DXF, or similar as requested on the form). State material, tolerances, quantities, and any critical features. CNC jobs may need design review before quoting. Complex setups or tight tolerances can affect price and schedule—include notes so our engineers can respond with the right process.

laserGuide.title: What should I know for laser jobs?
laserGuide.body: Laser cutting and engraving usually need clean vector outlines or approved file types (such as DXF, SVG, or PDF vectors) depending on the form. Specify material type and thickness, whether it is cut-through or surface engraving, and quantities. Kerf and finish can vary by material; if you are unsure, describe the outcome you need and we will advise.

materials.title: How does material selection work?
materials.body: Pick from the materials and options shown in the request or product flow. Those lists reflect what we can source and process for that service. If your application needs a special grade or certification, mention it in the notes. When two options seem close, prefer the one that matches your strength, temperature, or finish requirements—we can help you decide before production.

fileTypes.title: Which file types are supported for upload and preview?
fileTypes.body: For 3D geometry, STL is widely used for printing; STEP/STP and OBJ are common where the form allows them. CNC and mechanical work often use STEP or IGES solids. Laser vector work typically uses DXF, SVG, or PDF with usable vector data. Always follow the accept list on the uploader—if preview fails, re-export from your CAD tool with correct units and scale, and ensure meshes are watertight where required.

wrongFile.title: I uploaded the wrong file. What should I do?
wrongFile.body: Contact support as soon as possible with your request or order reference and attach the correct file if the process still allows changes. If production has not started, we can often swap files. If work has already begun or completed, options may be limited; our team will explain the next steps. Including clear notes when re-submitting reduces delays.

orderStatuses.title: What do order statuses mean?
orderStatuses.body: Pending: we received the order and are preparing it. Processing: production or fulfillment steps are underway. Shipped: the package has been handed to the carrier; use tracking if provided. Delivered: the carrier reports delivery—verify receipt. Cancelled: the order will not be completed per policy or your request. Exact labels may match your account language; statuses align with our operational stages.

payment.title: How do payments work?
payment.body: You pay at checkout for shop orders using the methods shown there. Charges are in the currency displayed for that checkout. If a payment fails, update your method or bank details and try again. Invoices or receipts, when issued, appear in your order or email as configured. For billing disputes or payment questions, contact support with your order number.

loginAccount.title: Login and account help
loginAccount.body: Use the same email and password you registered with. If you cannot sign in, use Forgot password and check spam folders for reset mail. Keep your profile and shipping addresses up to date under account settings so orders and notifications reach you correctly. For email verification issues, use resend options where available or contact support.

cancellationRefunds.title: Cancellations and refunds—basics
cancellationRefunds.body: Whether an order can be cancelled depends on status and whether production or shipping has started. Contact support promptly with your order number. Refunds, when applicable, are handled according to our policies and payment provider timelines. Custom or made-to-order work may have different rules than catalog items—we will explain options case by case.

designPrivacy.title: Are my 3D designs private? What is “My Designs”?
designPrivacy.body: Designs you upload while signed in are scoped to your account for quoting and production—they are not a public gallery for other customers. Use My Designs (or equivalent in your account) to manage files you have uploaded. Do not share account credentials; if you need a colleague to collaborate, use official sharing or contact options we provide. For data handling details, see our privacy policy.

cta.title: Still need help?
cta.description: Our team can clarify requirements, timelines, and account issues—reach out or start a new project.

footer.aboutDescription: MABA - Your trusted partner for quality products and services.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.body:
MABA Solutions respects your privacy and is committed to protecting any personal information you provide when using our website or services.

This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how it is used, and how it is protected.

informationWeCollect.title: Information We Collect
informationWeCollect.body:
When you use our website or contact us, we may collect the following information:

• Name
• Email address
• Phone number (if provided)
• Project details or service requests
• Uploaded files or design materials
• Technical information such as browser type and IP address

We only collect information necessary to provide our services.

howWeUse.title: How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.body:
The information collected may be used to:

• Respond to inquiries or service requests
• Provide engineering or manufacturing services
• Process orders and projects
• Improve website functionality and user experience
• Communicate updates related to your request

We do not sell or trade personal information to third parties.

fileConfidentiality.title: File and Design Confidentiality
fileConfidentiality.body:
Files, designs, or project materials submitted by customers are treated as confidential and used only for the purpose of fulfilling the requested service.

Customer files are not shared publicly without permission.

dataProtection.title: Data Protection
dataProtection.body: MABA Solutions uses reasonable security measures to protect stored information and prevent unauthorized access, disclosure, or misuse.

thirdPartyServices.title: Third-Party Services
thirdPartyServices.body: Our website may use third-party services such as hosting providers, payment processors, or analytics tools. These services may process limited information required to operate the website.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body:
Our website may use cookies or similar technologies to improve browsing experience and analyze website traffic.

Users may disable cookies through their browser settings if desired.

changesToPolicy.title: Changes to This Policy
changesToPolicy.body: MABA Solutions may update this Privacy Policy when necessary. Updates will be posted on this page.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
For privacy-related questions please contact:

MABA Solutions

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.body: By accessing or using the MABA Solutions website or services, you agree to comply with these Terms of Service.

useOfWebsite.title: Use of the Website
useOfWebsite.body: Users agree to use the website only for lawful purposes and not to misuse the platform or attempt to disrupt its operation.

services.title: Services
services.body:
MABA Solutions provides engineering services including but not limited to:

• Product design and development
• Embedded systems and electronics
• Software development
• Prototyping and manufacturing support
• Engineering consulting

Service availability and specifications may change as the company evolves.

customerFilesMaterials.title: Customer Files and Materials
customerFilesMaterials.body:
Customers may submit files, designs, or specifications required for project execution.

Customers confirm they have the right to use and share any files they submit.

MABA Solutions is not responsible for intellectual property violations caused by materials provided by customers.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.body:
All content on the MABA website including:

• Designs
• Graphics
• Software
• Documentation
• Branding

belongs to MABA Solutions unless otherwise stated.

Content may not be copied, reproduced, or used commercially without permission.

limitationOfLiability.title: Limitation of Liability
limitationOfLiability.body:
MABA Solutions provides services and information in good faith but cannot guarantee that the website or services will always be uninterrupted or error-free.

MABA is not responsible for damages resulting from misuse of provided products or services.

projectAgreements.title: Project Agreements
projectAgreements.body:
Certain projects may require separate agreements, quotations, or contracts that define scope, timelines, and responsibilities.

Those agreements take precedence over general website terms.

modifications.title: Modifications
modifications.body: MABA Solutions reserves the right to update or modify these Terms at any time. Updates will be reflected on this page.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
For questions regarding these Terms, please contact:

MABA Solutions

scopeOfServices.title: Scope of Services
scopeOfServices.body:
MABA Solutions provides engineering development, prototyping, and manufacturing services including software, embedded systems, mechanical design, and fabrication.

Each project may require a separate quotation and scope definition.

projectRequests.title: Project Requests
projectRequests.body: Submitting a request through the website does not constitute a final agreement. Projects begin only after scope confirmation and acceptance by both parties.

customerResponsibilities.title: Customer Responsibilities
customerResponsibilities.body:
Customers must ensure that all files, specifications, and information provided are accurate and complete.

Incorrect files or incomplete specifications may affect project results.

manufacturingTolerances.title: Manufacturing Tolerances
manufacturingTolerances.body:
Prototype manufacturing processes such as CNC machining, 3D printing, and laser fabrication involve natural tolerances and material limitations.

Minor variations may occur and are considered normal in prototype production.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.body:
Customers retain ownership of their submitted design files and intellectual property.

MABA Solutions retains ownership of its internal engineering methods, tools, software, and proprietary solutions.

confidentiality.title: Confidentiality
confidentiality.body: Project files and technical information provided by customers will be treated as confidential and used solely for the requested service.

liability.title: Liability
liability.body: MABA Solutions is not responsible for damages resulting from improper use of manufactured parts, prototypes, or engineering systems after delivery.

acceptance.title: Acceptance
acceptance.body: By submitting a project request or using MABA services, the customer acknowledges and agrees to these terms.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.body: MABA Solutions understands that many projects involve sensitive technical information, proprietary designs, and confidential product concepts. We are committed to protecting the confidentiality of materials submitted by our clients.

confidentialTreatment.title: Confidential Treatment of Files
confidentialTreatment.body:
All files, designs, drawings, specifications, and project documents submitted to MABA Solutions are treated as confidential engineering information.

These materials are used solely for the purpose of evaluating or fulfilling the requested service.

restrictedAccess.title: Restricted Access
restrictedAccess.body: Access to customer files is limited only to personnel involved in the technical evaluation, engineering development, or manufacturing process required for the project.

noPublicDisclosure.title: No Public Disclosure
noPublicDisclosure.body: MABA Solutions does not publish, distribute, or publicly display customer designs, prototypes, or project details without explicit permission from the customer.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.body:
Customers retain ownership of their submitted designs, files, and intellectual property.

MABA Solutions does not claim ownership over customer-created designs or engineering concepts.

optionalNda.title: Optional NDA Agreements
optionalNda.body: If required, customers may request a formal Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) prior to sharing sensitive materials.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.body: This Service Level Agreement outlines the general expectations for communication, project evaluation, and service delivery provided by MABA Solutions.

projectRequestReview.title: Project Request Review
projectRequestReview.body:
Project requests submitted through the website are reviewed by our engineering team.

Typical response time for initial review is within 1–3 business days.

quotationProcess.title: Quotation Process
quotationProcess.body:
After reviewing the project requirements, MABA Solutions may provide a quotation including estimated cost, scope, and timeline.

Quotation time may vary depending on project complexity.

productionTimeline.title: Production Timeline
productionTimeline.body:
Manufacturing and development timelines depend on several factors including:

• Project complexity
• Material availability
• Manufacturing processes involved
• Current workload

communication.title: Communication
communication.body: Customers may receive updates regarding project progress, quotation status, or required clarifications through email or website notifications.

technicalConsultation.title: Technical Consultation
technicalConsultation.body: For complex engineering projects, additional discussions may be required to clarify specifications, requirements, or feasibility.

serviceAvailability.title: Service Availability
serviceAvailability.body: Service availability may evolve as MABA Solutions expands its engineering capabilities and manufacturing infrastructure.

support.title: Support
support.body: Customers may contact MABA Solutions through the website contact form for questions related to services, projects, or support.

hero.title: Welcome to MABA
hero.subtitle: Your partner for engineering solutions

softwarePillar.title: Software Systems (A to Z)

workflow.title: How We Deliver
workflow.description: A proven methodology that ensures quality results.

step1.title: Consultation
step1.description: We discuss your requirements, goals, and constraints to understand your project fully.

step2.title: Design & Planning
step2.description: Our team creates detailed designs, specifications, and project timelines.

step3.title: Development
step3.description: We build and iterate, keeping you informed with regular updates and demos.

step4.title: Delivery & Support
step4.description: Final delivery with documentation, training, and ongoing support options.

direction.title: Our Direction
direction.intro: We are committed to advancing into medical technology and intelligent engineering systems that serve real needs.

bullets.medicalMachines: Medical machines and assistive technologies for healthcare providers.
bullets.smartDiagnostic: Smart diagnostic and monitoring systems for clinical environments.
bullets.embeddedHealthcare: Embedded healthcare-grade systems with real-time control.
bullets.precisionIntegration: Precision integration of hardware, firmware, and software.
bullets.realHumanProblems: Engineering systems that solve real human problems.

direction.callout: We are not building hobby products.
direction.standards: We are building systems that must meet high standards of safety, reliability, and performance.

medical.precisionAccountability: We understand that medical systems require precision and accountability.

hero.title: Precision CNC Routing & PCB Milling
hero.description: CNC fabrication for wood, plastics, and PCB prototyping with engineering-grade workflow.
hero.capability: Supported: wood, plastics, PCB | Metal machining is not supported.

materials.title: Select Your Material
materials.description: Choose from our range of CNC-compatible materials
materials.emptySubtitle: Contact us to discuss your specific requirements
materials.metalWarning: Metal machining is NOT supported on our CNC machines. Wood, plastics, and PCB only.
materials.metalWarningShort: Metal NOT supported. Wood, plastics, and PCB only.

operation.title: Operation Type
operation.cutHelper: Through-cut along paths. Provide vector outlines (DXF/SVG/PDF).
operation.engraveHelper: Surface marking only. No material removal depth.
operation.pocketHelper: Material removed inside an area to a specific depth.
operation.drillHelper: Drilling holes at points. Provide drill file or vector points.
operation.isolationHelper: Isolation routing to separate copper traces. Upload Gerber copper layer.
operation.pcbDrillHelper: Drill via and component holes. Upload Excellon drill file.
operation.cutoutHelper: Cut PCB outline/profile. Upload Gerber outline layer.
operation.textHelper: Mill text/silkscreen on PCB surface. Upload Gerber silk layer.
operation.drillHint: Or upload a drill file (.drl) with hole positions

pcbSide.title: PCB Sides
pcbSide.doubleWarning: Double-sided milling requires manual board alignment between top and bottom passes.

pcbThickness.title: Board Thickness
pcbThickness.hint: Standard PCB thickness is 1.6mm. Select based on your design requirements.

material.title: Material Selection
material.hint: Select a material to see operation constraints

pcbBanner.title: PCB Milling & Prototyping
pcbBanner.desc: Upload Gerbers or a design file and we'll mill your PCB prototype with isolation routing and drilling.

routingBanner.title: CNC Routing & Machining
routingBanner.desc: Upload your vector design (SVG, DXF, PDF) and we'll cut, engrave, pocket, or drill your wood and plastic parts.

designSource.label: Design Source:
designSource.processingRouting: We will vectorize your design and generate toolpaths.
designSource.processingPcb: We will convert your image or schematic into manufacturable Gerber files.

details.title: Material Details
details.selectMaterial: Select a material from the grid

upload.title: Design File
upload.hintRouting: Upload your design file (SVG, DXF, PDF)
upload.hintPcb: Upload your Gerber files (.zip or individual files)
upload.dropzone: Drop file here or click to browse
upload.manualWarning: MABA engineering team will convert and prepare your design. Final machining parameters will be confirmed during review.
upload.designNotesPlaceholder: Any details about colors, specific areas to cut, dimensions from sketch, etc.

cta.disclaimer: We will review your request and provide a quotation.
cta.sectionDescription: Our team is ready to help with your precision manufacturing needs.

caps.precisionRoutingDesc: High-accuracy cuts for wood and plastics
caps.pocketingDesc: Material removal for cavities and recesses
caps.pcbIsolationDesc: Trace isolation routing for PCB prototypes
caps.panelCuttingDesc: Profile cutting for enclosures and panels

routing.title: CNC Routing
routing.item1: Wood cutting and shaping (plywood, MDF, hardwood)
routing.item3: Profile cutting, pocketing, drilling, and engraving

routing.title: Routing Files
routing.item1: Formats: DXF, SVG, or PDF (vector only)
routing.item3: Paths must be closed for cutting operations

pcb.title: PCB Design Rules
pcb.alignmentNote: Double-sided boards require proper alignment marks for accurate registration.

useCases.panelsDesc: Custom control panels and device housings
useCases.enclosuresDesc: Precisely machined boxes for electronic devices
